_meta.purpose: Unified inventory of the Indian Liberals corpus. Single source of truth for what we have, where it lives, and what's been decided about it.
_meta.constraint_note: We are building an Astro site from scratch. The WordPress installation is a SOURCE of content; it is NOT a template, schema, or structural reference. Completeness is answered against the curator's drive (PDFs) + our schema, NOT against the legacy WP site.

content_collections_in_repo.primary_works_status: All 51 entries have needs_extraction: true. Stale OCR stripped; awaiting LLM extraction pipeline.
content_collections_in_repo.synthesis_collections_status: Empty. Populated by Phase 4 of LLM pipeline.

wp_db_dumps_extracted.current_wp: indianli_indianv3.sql (67 MB) — il_posts: 1,393 published content + 49 PDF attachments + 4 indian_liberals + 3 print
wp_db_dumps_extracted.mid_era_wp: indianli_liberals.sql (24 MB) — wp_author (11), wp_book (118), wp_av (27 YouTube), wp_periodicals (10), tbl_languages_details (51), tbl_languages_content (3,684 OCR pages — unusable)

wp_indian_liberals_post_type.what_they_are: Empty stub pages on the WP site that redirect to existing /content/<thinker-slug>/ pages. Pure WP navigation artefact.
wp_indian_liberals_post_type.decision: DROP. Each of the 4 thinkers is already in our authority file as canonical (a-d-shroff, m-r-pai, s-v-raju, raja-ram-mohan-roy). No content lost.

wp_print_post_type.what_they_are: ThePrint column previews — each just contains a description, byline, and a theprint.in URL. Already covered by our theprint-mirror collection (48 entries) by canonical slug.
wp_print_post_type.decision: DROP from custom-type extraction. The theprint-mirror collection is the canonical home for ThePrint columns; cross-verify the 'russian-style-socialism' entry exists in the mirror under a different slug or add it.

found_urls: https://indianliberals.in/about-us/ (Lorem ipsum body — no real content to migrate)
https://indianliberals.in/contact/ (Lorem ipsum body + canonical contact block: A-69 Hauz Khas, New Delhi 110016 / editor@indianliberals.in / +91-11-26521882)
static_pages_on_live_site.decision: About + Contact pages on the new Astro site are effectively green-field — no legacy prose to preserve. CCS editorial (Arjun + Kumar Anand) needs to write fresh copy. Contributors list can port verbatim. Editorial-policy / partners / how-to-contribute pages don't exist on the live site, so any such page on the new site is a net-new content decision.

polylang_translation_pairs.decision: Out of scope. We are not inheriting Polylang structure. Multilingual support on the new site uses our i18nFields shape (BCP-47 + translation_of + translations map). Existing translation relationships in the WP DB are not being migrated; the LLM extraction pipeline can detect translation_of relationships from PDF metadata where they exist.

audio_summarization.decision: Out of scope for this engagement. 27 audio files manifested in data/legacy-media.json. Future scope: audio-capable LLM (Gemini 2.5 Pro, GPT-5 audio, etc.) could transcribe + summarise them into interview entries with text bodies. Possible follow-up engagement.

thinker_biographies.decision: Deferred until corpus extraction completes and the canonical thinker roster is known. Authoring bios now is premature — we don't yet know all the figures the corpus surfaces. Post-extraction editorial work.

rasterization_smoke_test.rule: When rendering a chunk, detect blank pages (<0.1% non-white in grayscale) and render extra pages to fill the chunk. Cap extra at 1.5x requested.

subagent_image_input.test_case: Bengali 4-page Vidyasagar essay (balyo-bibaher-dosh) rendered to JPEGs, dispatched to general-purpose subagent with model: sonnet, returned structured JSON with Bengali script identified correctly
key_findings: Subagent can read N JPEGs via the Read tool (verified up to 4; production uses up to 20)
Sonnet vision handles Bengali script — validates premise P1 about Indic-language support
Subagent returns structured JSON in a fenced code block as instructed
Some PDFs have double-page spreads laid out as single PDF pages (visible in the test case); the LLM handles them but the rasterizer doesn't normalize
